rules.description: Attendance below 70%
rules.signal: Your attendance is below 70%.
rules.recommendation: Attend all remaining sessions — missing lectures compounds quickly.

rules.description: Attendance critically low (below 50%)
rules.signal: Your attendance is critically low — below 50%.
rules.recommendation: Contact your academic advisor immediately to discuss your attendance record.

rules.description: Low self-confidence
rules.signal: You rated your confidence at 2/5 or lower.
rules.recommendation: Break large tasks into smaller sub-goals to rebuild momentum.

rules.description: High stress level
rules.signal: Your stress level is high (4/5 or above).
rules.recommendation: Schedule at least one 30-minute break per study day. Consider speaking to a student wellbeing advisor.

rules.description: Extreme stress level
rules.signal: You reported maximum stress (5/5). This level significantly impairs study effectiveness.
rules.recommendation: Prioritise basic self-care today. Contact student wellbeing services — they can help re-plan workload.

rules.description: Quiz scores below 50%
rules.signal: Your average quiz / test score is below 50%.
rules.recommendation: Review lecture notes and past papers. Seek peer study groups or tutoring.

rules.description: Quiz scores very low (below 35%)
rules.signal: Your quiz / test scores are very low — below 35%. This is a serious academic signal.
rules.recommendation: Arrange an urgent meeting with your tutor or module coordinator.

rules.description: Urgent task with substantial hours
rules.signal: You have at least one task due within 7 days requiring more than 4 hours of work.
rules.recommendation: Start the most urgent task immediately — do not defer to later in the week.

rules.description: Three or more deadlines this week
rules.signal: You have 3 or more tasks due within 7 days — deadline cluster detected.
rules.recommendation: Triage tasks by deadline then hours needed. Contact instructors early if any cannot be completed.

rules.description: Exam within 4 days
rules.signal: You have an exam within 4 days.
rules.recommendation: Block at least 2 hours per day for exam revision. Reduce time on non-exam tasks until after the exam.

rules.description: Required study hours exceed free time
rules.signal: Your required study hours exceed the free time available this week.
rules.recommendation: Negotiate deadline extensions where possible, or reduce task scope with your instructor.

rules.description: Search planner failed to complete schedule
rules.signal: The search planner could not fit all tasks into your available time slots.
rules.recommendation: Drop or delay the lowest-urgency task. Add study time on weekend days if possible.

rules.description: Maximum credit load (80 credits)
rules.signal: You are carrying the maximum credit load — 4 modules (80 credits). Any disruption will cascade.
rules.recommendation: Identify which module is lowest priority and reduce effort there to protect higher-stakes work.

rules.description: Good attendance and high confidence (positive signal)
rules.signal: Good attendance (80%+) and high confidence — strong engagement signals.
rules.recommendation: Keep up the routine. Consider helping peers — teaching reinforces your own understanding.

rules.description: Overdue task present (past deadline)
rules.signal: You have at least one task whose deadline has already passed — marked OVERDUE.
rules.recommendation: Complete overdue work as soon as possible. Contact your instructor to discuss late submission options.

rules.description: Engagement risk: low attendance combined with low confidence
rules.signal: Low attendance combined with low confidence is a strong early-warning signal.
rules.recommendation: Visit office hours or a tutor to rebuild confidence. Attend all remaining sessions.

rules.description: Academic failure risk: very low attendance and very low quiz scores
rules.signal: Very low attendance combined with low quiz scores suggests a serious academic failure risk.
rules.recommendation: Arrange an urgent meeting with your module coordinator and student support.

rules.description: Critical overload: deadline cluster and schedule overload together
rules.signal: Multiple deadlines this week AND the planner cannot fit them all — critical overload confirmed.
rules.recommendation: Immediately triage: identify which task can be partially submitted or deferred. Communicate with instructors today.

rules.description: Deadline crunch: cluster of deadlines with insufficient free time
rules.signal: Multiple deadlines coincide with insufficient free hours — deadline crunch detected.
rules.recommendation: Add available study days (e.g. weekend) or negotiate at least one extension.

rules.description: Exam failure risk: imminent exam with low quiz performance
rules.signal: You have an imminent exam but current quiz scores suggest insufficient preparation.
rules.recommendation: Dedicate all available slots to exam revision. Skip non-exam tasks if necessary.

rules.description: Exam overload: imminent exam and schedule already overloaded
rules.signal: An imminent exam combined with an already overloaded schedule is a very high-risk combination.
rules.recommendation: Treat exam prep as the highest-priority task. Defer or drop other tasks to free up time.

rules.description: Burnout risk: high stress with poor quiz performance
rules.signal: High stress combined with low quiz scores may indicate burnout or disengagement.
rules.recommendation: Take a short planned break before continuing. Sustained stress reduces study effectiveness.

rules.description: Crisis risk: extreme stress with deadline cluster
rules.signal: Extreme stress combined with multiple imminent deadlines — this is a student crisis signal.
rules.recommendation: Contact student wellbeing services today. Academic performance cannot improve without wellbeing support.

rules.description: Paralysis risk: low confidence with urgent workload
rules.signal: Low confidence combined with urgent tasks may lead to avoidance behaviour.
rules.recommendation: Start with the smallest sub-task of the most urgent piece of work to break the paralysis.

rules.description: Overcommitted: maximum credits with high stress
rules.signal: Maximum credit load combined with high stress suggests you may be overcommitted this term.
rules.recommendation: Speak to your academic advisor about whether a module withdrawal is possible before the deadline.

rules.description: HIGH RISK: critical overload and engagement risk together
rules.signal: Critical overload and engagement risk together — highest risk category.
rules.recommendation: Immediate intervention required. Contact your academic tutor and student support today.

rules.description: HIGH RISK: academic failure risk signal
rules.signal: Academic failure risk confirmed — urgent action needed.
rules.recommendation: Arrange a meeting with your module coordinator this week.

rules.description: HIGH RISK: exam failure risk
rules.signal: Exam failure risk — insufficient preparation for an imminent exam.
rules.recommendation: Dedicate all available time to exam revision today and tomorrow.

rules.description: HIGH RISK: student crisis signal
rules.signal: Student crisis detected — extreme stress and deadline cluster.
rules.recommendation: Contact student wellbeing services. Academic support is available.

rules.description: HIGH RISK: schedule overload alone is sufficient for high risk
rules.signal: Schedule overload confirmed by the search planner — high risk of missing deadlines.
rules.recommendation: Review the overload recommendations generated by the planner.

rules.description: MEDIUM RISK: burnout or paralysis risk
rules.signal: Burnout risk detected — workload and stress are combining negatively.
rules.recommendation: Pace your study sessions and include planned breaks.

rules.description: MEDIUM RISK: deadline crunch
rules.signal: Deadline crunch — tight but possibly manageable with good prioritisation.
rules.recommendation: Use the A* schedule strictly. Avoid any non-essential commitments this week.

rules.description: MEDIUM RISK: overcommitted term
rules.signal: Overcommitted term detected — maximum credits with elevated stress.
rules.recommendation: Identify the module with the lowest grade weight and reduce effort there proportionally.

rules._comment: ===== CONFIRM (C*): positive signals -> trust, risk downgrade =====
rules.description: Good attendance confirmed
rules.signal: Strong attendance record (85%+) — consistent classroom presence confirmed.

rules.description: Strong academic performance confirmed
rules.signal: Quiz / test scores are strong (70%+) — good academic performance confirmed.

rules.description: Planner succeeded — schedule is feasible
rules.signal: The search planner found a complete, conflict-free schedule for all your tasks.
rules.recommendation: Follow the generated schedule. Re-run if a task or deadline changes.

rules.description: Trusted engagement: good engagement signal confirmed
rules.signal: Good engagement confirmed — high attendance and confidence working together.

rules.description: Low pressure: trusted engagement and feasible schedule
rules.signal: Trusted engagement and a feasible schedule together indicate a low-pressure week.
rules.recommendation: Keep up the routine and stay ahead of deadlines.

rules.description: Positive trajectory: strong attendance and academic performance
rules.signal: Strong attendance and quiz performance — positive academic trajectory confirmed.
rules.recommendation: Maintain this pace. Consider reviewing ahead of upcoming exam topics.

rules.description: DOWNGRADE: low_pressure warrants risk downgrade
rules.signal: Positive signals outweigh risk signals — risk level downgraded.
rules.recommendation: You are on track. Continue following your study plan.
